originalTitle: Inside the alleged Russian operation to trigger anti-government protests in Angola
title: Big News from Angola: Friends and Fairness!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Welcome to our super-duper news report, just for you! I'm your friendly news reporter, and today we're talking about something important happening in a beautiful country called Angola. It's like a big puzzle, and we're going to put the pieces together, nice and easy. Are you ready for an adventure in learning? Let's go!

What's Happening in Angola?

Imagine you are playing with your friends. Sometimes, someone might say something that makes others feel sad or upset. And sometimes, grown-ups have big discussions too. In Angola, there's a story about some grown-ups. Some people are saying that a group of people, from Angola and another country far away called Russia, tried to make other people feel upset. They are saying these people tried to make big groups gather and complain loudly. We call these big gatherings 'protests'. But the people who are being talked about? They are saying, "No, we didn't do that!" It's like when someone says you took their toy, but you know you didn't. So, what happens next? They are going to a special place called a 'court' to talk about it with wise judges. This is called a 'trial'.

Meet Angola, Our Beautiful Neighbour!

Angola is a wonderful country in Southern Africa. It has sunny beaches, amazing animals, and many friendly people. Just like your home, Angola has rules and people who help keep everything safe and fair. These people are called the 'government'. They work hard to make sure schools are built, roads are smooth, and everyone has a chance to live happily. Did you know? Angola is famous for its diamonds! These are sparkling stones found deep in the earth. Imagine finding a tiny star in the ground! Angola also has a special giant sable antelope, which is a very rare and beautiful animal with long, curved horns.

What Are 'Protests'?

Sometimes, grown-ups feel very strongly about something. Maybe they think a rule isn't fair, or they want the government to listen to their ideas more. When many people feel the same way, they might gather together. They might hold signs or sing songs to show everyone how they feel. This is called a 'protest'. It's like when you and your friends want to play a new game, but the teacher says no. You might all ask nicely together, saying, "Please, can we try the new game?" That's a bit like a protest, but for grown-ups and bigger things. Protests are important because they help people share their voices. But it's also very important that protests are peaceful and don't make trouble or hurt anyone.

Who Are the People in Our Story?

In our story, there are people from Angola, which is our African neighbour. And there are also people from a country called Russia. Russia is very, very far away, across oceans and many lands. It's a huge country with different kinds of weather, like lots of snow! Sometimes, people from different countries visit each other, work together, or even live in other lands. That's what happened here. Did you know? Russia is the largest country in the world! It's so big that it crosses two continents: Europe and Asia. That's like having a house so big it's in two different towns!

What is 'Being Accused' and a 'Trial'?

When someone says, "You did this bad thing!" but it's not proven yet, we say that person is 'accused'. It doesn't mean they are guilty, it just means someone is saying they did something. It's like if your sibling says, "You ate all the cookies!" You are accused, but maybe you didn't! To find out the truth, people go to a 'trial'. A trial is a special meeting with wise judges and lawyers. Everyone gets a chance to tell their side of the story. The judges listen very carefully to all the evidence, which are like clues. They try to figure out what really happened. It's very important to be fair to everyone. Did you know? In a trial, there's a person called a 'judge' who makes sure everyone follows the rules and helps find the truth. They are like a referee in a football game, making sure the game is fair for both teams!

Why Is This Important?

This story is important because it's about fairness and peace. Everyone in Angola, and all over Africa, wants to live in peace and happiness. When people try to cause trouble, it can make others sad or even scared. We all need to feel safe in our homes and our countries. It's important that people can share their ideas, but also that we don't let anyone trick others into making trouble. The trial will help everyone understand what happened and make sure that justice is served. Justice means doing what is right and fair for everyone.

Working Together for a Happy Africa!

In Africa, we are like a big family. We help each other, we learn from each other, and we want everyone to be happy and safe. This story reminds us that it's good to be careful and to always ask questions if something doesn't feel right. We must always choose peace and fairness. Did you know? Africa is home to the fastest land animal, the cheetah! It can run super-fast, just like how quickly good news can spread across our continent!

Our Future is Bright!

Even with these big grown-up stories, remember that Angola is a strong country. Its people are resilient and work hard for a beautiful future. We can all learn from this story to be kind, to speak up if we see something unfair, and to always choose peace. Keep smiling, amazing kids of Africa, and stay curious! Until next time!

originalTitle: Nigerian police arrest 15 after viral videos of alleged sexual assault at festival
title: Nigerian police arrest 15 after viral videos of alleged sexual assault at festival
content: The videos sparked widespread anger, leading to the hashtag #StopRapingWomen to trend on social media.

originalTitle: Kenyan ex-foreign minister arrested and accused of staging his disappearance
title: A Big Mystery Solved in Kenya!
content:
Hi everyone! It's your friendly kid news reporter here, coming to you from beautiful Africa! Today, we have a super interesting story from a country called Kenya. Kenya is a lovely place with big animals like lions and elephants! We're talking about a man named Mr. Raphael Tuju. He used to be a very important helper for his country, like a big boss who helps the President make important decisions for everyone.

A Man Goes Missing!

Imagine playing hide-and-seek, but your friend goes missing for real and everyone gets very worried! That's kind of what happened with Mr. Tuju. One day, people couldn't find him. He just wasn't where he was supposed to be. When someone important like Mr. Tuju can't be found, it's a big deal. People were wondering, 'Where did Mr. Tuju go?' His friends and family were sad and worried. They looked everywhere, but he was nowhere to be seen for a little while. Did you know? Kenya is famous for its amazing wildlife safaris! You can see giraffes with long necks and zebras with cool stripes there. It's a land of adventure and beautiful landscapes!

People Were Worried!

Because Mr. Tuju was an important person, some people thought something bad might have happened to him. They thought maybe someone had taken him away! This is called being 'abducted.' It means someone takes a person away not nicely, when they don't want to go. It's a scary thought, isn't it? When a grown-up goes missing, it makes everyone feel a bit nervous and scared. The news talked about it, and everyone hoped Mr. Tuju would be found safe and sound. People talked about it at home, at school, almost everywhere in Kenya. Did you know? Ministers, like Mr. Tuju used to be, are like big helpers to the President of a country. They help make sure things like roads, schools, and hospitals work well for everyone! They have important jobs to do.

A Big Surprise from the Police!

But then, something amazing happened! The police, who are like super helpers who keep us safe and solve mysteries, found Mr. Tuju! Everyone was happy that he was safe. But then, the police shared a big surprise that made everyone go 'Whoa!' The police said that Mr. Tuju was 'arrested.' When someone is arrested, it means the police want to talk to them about something important and serious. They usually take them to a police station to ask questions and try to understand what happened. Did you know? Police officers wear uniforms and often drive special cars with flashing lights! Their job is to help people, make sure everyone follows the rules, and keep our communities safe from bad things happening.

Did He Pretend to Be Lost?

And here's the super surprising part! The police said Mr. Tuju was 'accused' of something. Accused means people are saying he did something wrong, and they have proof or reasons to think so. And what was he accused of? They said he might have 'staged his disappearance'! What does 'staged his disappearance' mean? It means he might have pretended to be missing! Like when you play pretend, but in real life and it's a serious situation. It means he knew where he was all along, but he made it seem like he was gone, and everyone got worried for no real reason. Imagine you hide really, really well in hide-and-seek, and everyone thinks you're truly lost, but you're just giggling behind a curtain! It's a bit like that, but for grown-ups and a much more serious thing that can cause a lot of trouble. Did you know? Telling the truth is always the best thing to do! Even if it's hard, it helps everyone trust each other and keeps things simple and honest. Pretending can sometimes cause big problems!

Why Would Someone Pretend?

This is the big question everyone is asking! Why would Mr. Tuju pretend to be lost? The police are now trying very hard to find out the real reason. They will ask many questions to understand what happened and why he did it. Sometimes, grown-ups do strange things. Maybe they want to hide from something or someone, or maybe they want people to feel sorry for them. We don't know why Mr. Tuju might have done this, but the police want to find out the truth for everyone. The police have a very important job to do when something like this happens. They need to find out what really happened, step by step. It's like solving a big puzzle! They collect clues and talk to people to understand the whole story and make sure justice is done. Did you know? Africa is a giant continent with many different countries, languages, and cultures! Kenya is just one of its beautiful gems, known for its friendly people and amazing natural wonders.

What Happens Next?

Now, the police will continue their 'investigation.' This means they will keep looking into everything very carefully, asking more questions, and gathering all the facts. They want to know the real reason why Mr. Tuju disappeared and why he was found in such a surprising way. If someone is found to have done something wrong after a long investigation, they might have to go to a special place called a 'court.' In court, a judge listens to all the facts and arguments from everyone and decides what is fair and what should happen next. It's a serious situation, but it's important that we understand the truth. We hope that soon, everyone will know the full story of Mr. Tuju's disappearance and why the police say he might have pretended to be gone. So, there you have it! A big mystery from Kenya, with a very surprising turn. It shows us that sometimes, things are not always what they seem. And it reminds us that the police work hard every day to find out the truth and keep us all safe and sound. This is your kid-friendly news reporter, signing off from Africa! Stay curious and always ask questions!
summary: A man in Kenya, Mr. Tuju, was thought to be missing, but then police found him and say he might have pretended to disappear, and now they are investigating why.
whyMatters: This story is important because it teaches us that things are not always what they seem, and that telling the truth is super important, plus the police work hard to find out what really happened and keep everyone safe and honest.

originalTitle: Despite Trump’s peace talk claims, US-Israeli attacks continue to hit Iran
title: News from Far Away: Let's Hope for Peace!
content:
Hello, Amazing Children of Africa!

Hello, hello, my wonderful young friends! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some important news with you today. I hope you are all having a super sunny day, playing nicely with your friends and family. Today, we're going to talk about something happening very far away, but it's important for all of us to understand, even if it sounds a little bit tricky.

Loud Noises in a Faraway Land

Imagine you are playing in your yard or going to school. Suddenly, you hear a very, very loud noise! It might make you feel a little bit scared or worried, right? Well, that's what happened in a country very far from Africa. This country is called Iran. People in cities there heard some big, loud noises. This made many people feel sad and worried. Did you know? Iran is a country in Asia, a very big continent! It has beautiful mountains and deserts, and many friendly people live there.

Countries Not Playing Nicely

Sometimes, just like friends on the playground, big countries can have disagreements. They might not agree on how to share things, or what rules everyone should follow. When countries don't agree, they sometimes don't play nicely together. That's what has been happening with Iran and some other big countries like America and Israel.

Talking About Friendship

When you and your friend have a disagreement, what do you do? You talk, right? You try to find a way to make things better so you can play together again. Well, countries try to do that too! They try to have 'talks' or meetings to find a way to be friends again and stop any disagreements from getting bigger. Some people, like the leader of America, were hoping that everyone was talking about making peace. Peace means everyone is safe, happy, and living without fear. It means countries are friends and help each other, just like good neighbors.

When Talks Don't Work Yet

The news we heard was that even though some people thought countries were talking about peace, the loud noises happened again in Iran. This is a bit like when you try to sort out a fight with your friend, but then the fighting starts again. It means they are still not playing nicely, and the 'talks' did not work to stop the loud noises this time. It makes us feel a little bit sad because we all want countries to be friends and live in peace. We want everyone, everywhere, to feel safe and happy, just like you do in your homes. Did you know? The opposite of peace is conflict, which means people or countries are fighting. Nobody likes conflict because it can hurt people and make them sad.

Why is Peace So Important?

Think about your school. If everyone was fighting all the time, it wouldn't be a happy place, would it? You couldn't learn properly, and you would always be worried. It's the same for countries. When there is peace, children can go to school, families can grow food, and everyone can build happy lives. Peace helps people smile and dream big dreams. Did you know? Many amazing people in history have worked very hard for peace. One famous person was Nelson Mandela from South Africa, who taught us about forgiveness and togetherness.

Hoping for a Brighter Tomorrow

Even though we heard sad news about loud noises, we must always hope for peace. Every child, every family, and every country deserves to live in peace and safety. We hope that Iran, America, Israel, and all countries can find a way to be friends. We hope they can keep talking and find good solutions, just like you do with your friends.

What Can We Do?

Even though we are small, we can do big things! We can be kind to our friends. We can share our toys. We can help our classmates. When we show kindness and friendship in our own lives, we are helping to build a more peaceful world, one smile at a time. Every act of kindness is like a little seed of peace that can grow into something big and beautiful. Did you know? Africa has many different cultures and languages, but we all share the dream of peace and friendship! So, let's keep hoping for a bright, peaceful, and happy future for all the children and people in Iran, and everywhere else in the world. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends! Stay safe and keep smiling! Your friendly reporter will be back with more news soon!
summary: Loud noises were heard in a faraway country called Iran, making people worried, even though some hoped big countries were talking to make peace, showing they are still having disagreements.
whyMatters: This news matters because when countries disagree and make loud noises, people can feel scared, and it reminds us how important it is for everyone to be friends and live in peace.

originalTitle: Top EU official calls for negotiations with Iran and an end to war
title: Let's Talk, Not Fight! A Big Leader Wants Peace!
content:
Hello, Wonderful Kids of Africa!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here. I have some super important news to share with you today. It's about something called 'peace'. Do you know what peace is? It's when everyone is happy and safe, and no one is fighting. It's like when you play nicely with your friends in the playground! Today, we are talking about a very big leader from far away. She wants more peace in the world. Isn't that wonderful news?

Who is Ursula? A Very Important Lady!

Her name is Ursula. Ursula Von der Leyen. That's a big name, right? She is like a very important head teacher for many, many countries in a place called Europe. Europe is another big land far away from Africa. Think of it like a big, big school, and she helps lead all the classrooms, which are countries. Ursula is the President of the European Commission. This means she works very hard to make sure all the countries in Europe work together. She wants everyone to be safe and happy. She cares a lot about peace for everyone. Did you know? There are seven big lands on Earth called continents! Africa is one, and Europe is another. They are like giant puzzle pieces of our planet!

What is 'Negotiation'? It Means Talking!

Ursula has asked for something called 'negotiations'. It sounds like a grown-up word, doesn't it? But it's actually very simple. 'Negotiations' just means talking. It means sitting down and talking about problems instead of shouting or fighting. Imagine you and your friend both want to play with the same toy. Instead of pulling the toy and getting angry, you can 'negotiate'. You can talk. Maybe you say, "You play with it for 5 minutes, then I play with it for 5 minutes." That's negotiating! You talk and find a happy way to share. Talking helps us understand each other. When people talk nicely, they can find solutions. They can make good plans together. It's much better than fighting, don't you think? Did you know? When you talk out a problem with your friends or family, you are doing a mini-negotiation! You are making peace in your own small world.

Iran: A Country Far Away

Ursula wants these important talks, these 'negotiations', with a country called Iran. Iran is also a country far, far away from us, like Europe. Sometimes countries have big disagreements. Just like sometimes friends have a hard time agreeing. When countries have disagreements, sometimes things can get tricky. People might not be happy. Ursula thinks talking is the best way to make things better between countries, like Iran and others. She believes that talking can help everyone understand each other more. And understanding is the first step to being friends again, or at least being peaceful neighbours.

No More Fighting! Let's Have Peace!

Another very important thing Ursula said is that she wants an "end to hostilities." 'Hostilities' is another big word. It means fighting. It means when people or countries are not getting along at all. It means there is war. War is very, very sad. When there is war, people get hurt. Homes can be broken. Children cannot go to school. People have to leave their homes. It is a very scary time. Ursula wants all fighting to stop. She wants peace. Peace means safety. Peace means children can play. Peace means families can be together. Peace means everyone can learn and grow strong. Did you know? A white dove, a type of bird, is a famous symbol of peace all over the world! Also, an olive branch, from an olive tree, means peace too. They remind us to choose peace.

Why Peace is Good for Everyone, Even You!

Why is this news about peace important for you, wonderful kids in Africa? Because peace everywhere is good for everyone. When countries fight, it makes the whole world a little bit sadder. But when countries talk and find peace, it makes the whole world brighter! When there is peace in other parts of the world, it means more people can help each other. It means everyone can focus on making life better, like building schools, finding new medicines, and helping people grow food. Imagine if everyone in the world always talked nicely and helped each other. What a happy place that would be! Your leaders in Africa also work very hard for peace in our own continent and with other countries. We all want a world where children like you can grow up safe and happy. Did you know? Children from every country, every continent, wish for peace. We all want to live in a world where everyone is kind to each other.

Leaders Work Hard for a Happy World

So, Ursula, the important lady from Europe, is asking everyone to choose talking over fighting. She wants leaders to sit down and work things out. She wants an end to all the sad fighting. It reminds us that even when we have small disagreements, talking is always the best way. Be like Ursula! Choose to talk. Choose to share. Choose to be kind. Let's all cheer for peace! Hooray for talking! Hooray for making the world a safer, happier place for all children, everywhere, including you, my amazing friends in Africa!
summary: A very important leader from Europe, Ursula Von der Leyen, wants countries to talk instead of fight to bring peace and end conflicts.
whyMatters: This is important because when leaders talk instead of fight, it makes the world a safer and happier place for everyone, including children in Africa, allowing us all to learn, play, and grow without fear.

originalTitle: Eritrea to end 18-year AFCON isolation by playing Eswatini in qualifier
title: Eritrea to end 18-year AFCON isolation by playing Eswatini in qualifier
content: Eritrea vs Eswatini on Wednesday will serve as the first AFCON qualifier for the east African nation since 2008.

originalTitle: Ultimatums, diplomacy and a trip to Graceland as Trump eyes a deal with Iran
title: Talking for Peace: America's Leader!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! It's your news friend here, ready to share some exciting stories from around the world. Today, we're going to talk about something very important: how big leaders from big countries try to make friends and solve problems. It's just like when you talk to your friends to make sure everyone is happy and playing nicely.

Meet the Leader of America!

Far, far away, across the big blue ocean, there is a very big country called America. America has a special leader. We call him the President. He is like the head teacher of the whole country! His job is to make sure everyone in America is safe and happy. He also talks to leaders from other countries. This is a very big job! Did you know? America is a very, very big country! It's so big that it has many, many cities and lots of different kinds of people. Our news today is about this President. Sometimes, big countries have big problems or disagreements with other big countries. It's just like when you and your friend might disagree about which game to play. Instead of shouting or getting upset, what do you do? You talk!

Talking to Make Friends (That's Diplomacy!)

The President of America has been doing a lot of talking. When leaders from different countries talk to each other to solve problems, we call this "diplomacy." It's a fancy word, but it just means talking kindly and trying to understand each other. Imagine you have two friends, and they are not getting along. A good leader helps them talk. They help them find a way to be friends again. That's exactly what diplomacy is! It's about finding good solutions without fighting. It's about making peace. Did you know? When leaders talk, they often sit at a big table. They share ideas. Sometimes they even share a meal! It's a way to build trust. The President wants to make sure America is safe. And he wants to make sure other countries are safe too. So, he talks to many people. He sends messages. He makes calls. All this is part of his big job to keep the peace.

A Faraway Land Called Iran

There is another country, also very far away from America, called Iran. Sometimes, America and Iran have had some disagreements. It's like when two children might not understand each other's games. The President of America wants to talk to the leaders of Iran. He hopes that by talking, they can understand each other better. He hopes they can solve any problems they have. This is a very brave thing to do! It takes a lot of effort to talk when things are not easy. Did you know? Iran is a country with a very long history. It has beautiful old buildings and wonderful stories from many, many years ago! Talking helps everyone. When countries talk, they can learn from each other. They can share ideas. They can even work together to help people. The President is trying to build bridges, not walls, between countries. He wants everyone to live in peace.

Sometimes Leaders Do Fun Things Too!

Even though the President has a very serious job, he also gets to do other things. Our news tells us that sometimes, he even takes trips to special places. One time, he went to a very famous house called Graceland. Graceland is a special house in America. It used to belong to a very famous singer named Elvis Presley. People loved his music! It was a fun trip. Did you know? Elvis Presley was called "The King of Rock and Roll!" He wore sparkling clothes and sang songs that made everyone want to dance! It shows us that even big leaders with big jobs need a break sometimes. They are people just like us! They like to see new places and enjoy fun things. But even on these trips, they are always thinking about their country and how to make things better.

Why Is All This Talking Important for Everyone?

You might ask, "Why does this matter to me, a child in Africa?" That's a great question! It matters a lot. When big countries talk and find ways to be friends, it makes the whole world a safer place. When there is peace, people can go to school. They can play outside. They can grow food. When leaders talk and solve problems, it means less worry for everyone. It means more chances for all of us to learn and grow. Did you know? When countries are friends, they can help each other with things like medicines, food, and even building new schools! So, the President's talking is a way to make sure that children everywhere, including you, can live in a world that is calm and full of good things. He is trying to make sure that smiles are everywhere, not frowns.

What We Can Learn From This!

This story teaches us a big lesson. It teaches us that talking is always the best way. When you have a problem with a friend or a brother or sister, remember the President. Remember that he tries to talk things out. Talking helps us understand each other. It helps us forgive. And it helps us build strong, happy friendships. Just like the President is trying to build friendships between countries. So, let's all try to be good talkers! Let's listen to each other. Let's try to understand. Because when we talk, we can make our homes, our schools, our towns, and even our big, beautiful continent of Africa, a happier place. And who knows? Maybe one day, one of you will be a great leader. And you will remember to always choose talking and kindness. Thank you for listening, my friends! Stay curious, stay kind, and always keep talking!
summary: The leader of America, called the President, is talking to leaders from other countries, like Iran, to solve problems and make sure everyone can live in peace, sometimes even taking fun trips like to Graceland.
whyMatters: When big leaders talk instead of fight, it helps make the whole world safe and happy for children to learn and play, just like it's important for us to talk to our friends.

originalTitle: 'Like the plane got cut in half': LaGuardia crash that killed two pilots now under investigation
title: A Big Bump, Super Seatbelts, and Learning to Fly Safe!
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hello, amazing friends from all over Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here. I'm so happy to talk to you today. We love to learn new things, don't we? Today, we are going to talk about something important. It is about airplanes and staying safe. Airplanes are wonderful! They take us to see family. They let us visit new places. Sometimes, they even carry important things around the world. We all dream of flying high in the sky!

What Happened Far Away?

Sometimes, things happen that make us think about safety. There was a special airplane. It was an Air Canada plane. This plane was at a very big airport. The airport is in a city called New York. New York is far, far away in a country called America. At this airport, a plane had a little bump. It touched a big fire truck. This happened by accident. It was not on purpose. Everyone was a bit surprised. This kind of bump is very rare. Planes usually fly safely all the time. Did you know? Airplanes are one of the safest ways to travel! Millions of people fly every single day.

Helpers at the Airport!

Even though it was a surprising bump, many people helped. Airports have very brave helpers. They are called firefighters. Firefighters drive big, red trucks. Their job is to keep everyone safe. They are always ready to help if there is a problem. In this story, a fire truck was part of the bump. But it reminds us that firefighters are always there. They are super heroes! They work hard to protect planes and people. They train for many, many years to be good at their job. Did you know? Airport firefighters have special trucks that can spray a lot of water very fast!

Why Seatbelts Are Superheroes!

Now, here is something super important. Most of the people on the plane were okay. This is because of something small but mighty: seatbelts! Do you know what a seatbelt is? A seatbelt is like a strong hug. It holds you tight in your seat. When you are in a car or a plane, you fasten your seatbelt. This keeps you safe. If there is a sudden stop or a bump, your seatbelt keeps you safe in your seat. A grown-up named Sean Duffy talked about this. He said seatbelts helped save many lives. That means many people were safe because they wore their seatbelts. So, always remember to buckle up! It's super important. Did you know? Seatbelts were invented a very long time ago to keep people safe!

What is an 'Investigation'?

When something surprising like this happens, grown-ups want to understand why. They start something called an 'investigation'. An investigation is like solving a big puzzle. Smart people look at everything. They look at the plane. They look at the fire truck. They talk to people who saw what happened. They want to find out all the answers. Why did the plane and the truck bump? What can we learn from this? The reason for an investigation is always to make things safer for everyone. They want to make sure it never happens again. It is a very important job. Did you know? Planes have special recorders called 'black boxes'. These boxes help investigators understand what happened during a flight!

How Do Planes Fly? (A Little Bit!)

Have you ever looked up and seen a plane flying high? It's amazing, isn't it? Airplanes are big machines. They have wings. The wings help the plane lift up into the sky. They also have big engines. These engines push the plane forward very fast. Pilots are the special grown-ups who fly the planes. They know all about how planes work. They train for many, many years to fly safely. They are very clever and careful. Flying a plane is a big responsibility. Did you know? Birds helped people get the idea to build airplanes! Their wings are like plane wings.

Meet the Airport Team!

An airport is like a tiny city! Many people work there to help planes fly safely. We already talked about pilots and firefighters. But there are more! There are ground crew helpers. They guide the planes on the ground. They load bags. There are also air traffic controllers. These special people sit in a tall tower. They talk to pilots. They tell planes when it's safe to take off or land. Everyone works together like a big team. Did you know? The control tower at an airport is like the eyes and ears of the airport. They can see everything!

Learning About Safety!

So, what can we learn from this story? We learn that even though planes are mostly very safe, we must always be careful. Listen to the grown-ups who work at the airport. They tell us important things. Always remember to fasten your seatbelt. It is like your own personal safety shield! It helps keep you safe in your seat. And know that many smart and brave people work hard every day to make flying safe for you and me. Flying is a wonderful adventure. And we want to make sure every adventure is a safe one. So next time you fly, give a little wave to the airport team! And buckle up tight! Did you know? Every plane gets checked very carefully before it flies, just like you check your bicycle before you ride it!

Flying Safely Together!

Planes connect our world. They help us see our beautiful continent of Africa. They help us learn about other places. We can all do our part to stay safe. Listen, learn, and always remember your seatbelt! Thank you for listening, my dear friends. Until next time, stay curious and stay safe!
summary: An airplane had a small bump with a fire truck in a faraway city, but many people stayed safe thanks to their seatbelts, and now grown-ups are investigating to learn how to make flying even safer for everyone.
whyMatters: This story helps us learn that listening to safety rules, especially wearing our seatbelts, is super important to keep us safe when we travel, and that many clever people work hard to make sure planes fly safely all the time.

originalTitle: Inside the alleged Russian operation to trigger anti-government protests in Angola
title: Inside the alleged Russian operation to trigger anti-government protests in Angola
content: A group of Russians and Angolans are going on trial in Angola after being accused of fomenting protests, which they deny.

originalTitle: Dirt pitches, dodging dogs and choosing Chelsea - the making of Estevao
title: Dirt pitches, dodging dogs and choosing Chelsea - the making of Estevao
content: From the dirt pitch to practising against a rottweiler and Thiago Silva's advice - the foundations of Chelsea forward Estevao's rise.

originalTitle: Football's great entertainers - ranking the biggest showboaters
title: Football's great entertainers - ranking the biggest showboaters
content: We all love to watch the players with the flicks and tricks, the mavericks with the magic manoeuvres. Here, BBC Sport ranks football's top showboaters.

originalTitle: Depay explains using mobile phone during game
title: Depay explains using mobile phone during game
content: Corinthians striker Memphis Depay explains why he was using a mobile phone while on the substitutes' bench during a Brazilian top-flight match.
